reason: MISLEADING DESCRIPTION OF FORCE DENSITY CASE (line 378-379): The proof states that equation (conservation-force) 'follows by the same argument without discarding the boundary term.' This is misleading. In the integration by parts, the boundary terms [p^alpha(tau) phi(gamma(tau))] at tau -> +/-infinity STILL vanish by the same properness+compact-support argument -- regardless of whether dp^alpha/dtau is zero or not. The boundary terms do not contribute to G^alpha. What actually produces the non-zero G^alpha is that dp^alpha/dtau \!= 0 in the remaining integral after integration by parts, so the integral in eq (cons-ibp) no longer vanishes. The phrasing 'without discarding the boundary term' incorrectly implies that the boundary terms are non-zero in the force case and contribute to the result. A correct description would be: 'follows by the same argument; when dp^alpha/dtau \!= 0, the integral in (cons-ibp) no longer vanishes and gives the force density G^alpha.' This is not merely a cosmetic issue: a reader following the stated logic would incorrectly conclude that G^alpha arises from boundary contributions.
